title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Repository Trace: Restore Working Index
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git restore working index increment in an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Restore Working Index
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Restore Working Index contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Restore Working Index condition decided your prediction.

options.text: git restore always recovers content from the last commit. This does not resolve predict restore working index.

For predict restore working index, apply Restore Working Index without its stated preconditions, omit the an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Restore Working Index for an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.
Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Restore Working Index while producing an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer. Competency probe: Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs. A defensible solution applies Restore Working Index, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-restore-working-index — Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
git status --short
git branch recovery/before-repair
git diff
git reflog -n 4
# Choose restore, revert, or reset only after naming the affected layer.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
